steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The word “However” sets up a contrast: the three‑host CLP’s abundance dropped with rising temperature while the directly transmitted parasite’s abundance stayed stable.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B captures this contrast by saying the advantages of the three‑host transmission strategy did not completely offset the negative temperature effects, which matches the observed drop.
The other three choices either claim CLPs were less dependent on affected hosts, claim directly transmitted parasites were more shielded, or claim more hosts give better protection—all of which conflict with the data.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
